name: Theory & Mathematics
description: Core vocabulary, design thinking, geometry, and mathematical principles underlying architectural form.

name: Building Materials & Construction
description: The physical substances and methods used to build structures.

name: Structure & Physics
description: How buildings stand up — forces, frames, load paths, and the physical behaviour of buildings.

name: Design Styles & Movements
description: Architectural periods, styles, and the ideas that shaped them.

name: Tools & Technology
description: Digital and physical tools used in architectural practice.

name: Sustainability & Urban Design
description: Green design, energy performance, ecological thinking, cities, and the built environment at scale.

name: History & Cultures
description: Building types across time, traditions, vernacular forms, and regional architectures worldwide.

name: Key Figures
description: Architects, theorists, and thinkers who shaped the discipline.

name: Practice & Standards
description: How architecture works as a profession — process, contracts, roles, regulations, and compliance frameworks.

name: Representation & Communication
description: Drawing, modelling, and presenting architectural ideas.
